p.strong: Step-by-step decision tree — valid data vs. real error

g.text: State Comparison: Same word=-1, Different errno

div.p: BUG: string containing 0xFF 0xFF 0xFF 0xFF 0xFF 0xFF 0xFF 0xFF followed by \0 causes premature abort — word == -1 but it's VALID DATA!
PTRACE_PEEKDATA(addr) returns word = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF = -1L This is valid string data, not an error! Next word at addr+8 contains the null terminator \0.

g.text: Step 1: Clear errno IMMEDIATELY before call

p.em: (no function calls between here and ptrace)

g.text: Step 3: TWO-CONDITION check — both must be true for real error

ul.li: Permission denied (EPERM = 1) errno stays 0 when word is legitimately -1

div.p: ⚠️ CRITICAL: No function calls between errno = 0 and ptrace(). Even fprintf() can set errno!
Before: errno = 0 After: word = -1L, errno = 0 → errno unchanged by ptrace → Kernel did NOT set errno → -1L is valid tracee data → Continue; scan byte[0..7] as 0xFF
Before: errno = 0 After: word = -1L, errno = 14 (EFAULT) → errno WAS changed by ptrace → Kernel reported an error → -1L is an error sentinel → Abort; return -1 to caller
